rationale: The subject roof within the orange bounding box is a large, complex multi-gabled/hip-style home with what appears to be dark charcoal asphalt shingles. Across all visible roof planes — including the multiple hip sections, valleys, and dormers — the shingle coloration is consistent and uniform with no large-scale discoloration, staining streaks, or color-mismatched patches visible. There are no tarps, blue or brown coverings, or foreign debris present. No biological growth (moss or algae) is detectable on any slope. Ridge lines appear geometrically straight without visible sag, consistent with neighboring homes of similar construction. Lighter tonal variations in some areas are attributable to sunlight angle differences across the varied roof faces rather than any surface damage. No findings met the confidence threshold to warrant inclusion. The roof presents as visually intact at this resolution and image quality is sufficient for a reliable assessment.
